title: The One With Rachels Assistant
weakest_section: 08:00-09:00 range with setup but fewer payoffs

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Well, how come I can't get my VCR to stop blinking 12:00?
jokes.setup: Cheese calls Mac a genius
jokes.punchline: Mac reveals he can't figure out basic VCR programming
jokes.explanation: Classic self-deprecating humor that undermines the genius claim with relatable tech incompetence

jokes.text: That was one of the worst things ever... and not just on TV.
jokes.setup: Characters trying to give polite feedback about Joey's show
jokes.punchline: Monica escalates beyond TV criticism to universal awfulness
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalation that lands the harsh truth with perfect economy

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, you know, the lighting was... interesting.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And your delivery in that scene was very... committed.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Look, Joey, I'm just going to say it. You were terrible. Absolutely terrible. The worst thing I've ever seen. And I once watched Chandler try to parallel park for forty minutes.

jokes.text: You got lighting last time. Lighting is mine.
jokes.setup: Friends dividing up fake compliments to give Joey
jokes.punchline: Ross's petty territorial claim over 'lighting' as his designated lie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross behavior - making petty rules about meaningless things

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I call lighting. That's my compliment. You guys can have the rest, but the lighting thing is mine.

jokes.text: I'm just gonna press my breasts up against him.
jokes.setup: Monica can't lie to Joey about his show
jokes.punchline: Her solution is physical distraction rather than honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Monica's competitive, solution-oriented mind applied to an absurd situation

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, I have something to tell you about your audition.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? Did I get it?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, no, but—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But what?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm suddenly very attracted to you right now.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm.

jokes.text: You mean behind my back?
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks if his favorite animal says much about him
jokes.punchline: Joey interprets it as literal animal gossip
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey logic - perfect misdirection with economy and character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What's your favorite animal say about you?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, it says I'm a really great guy and that I'm incredibly handsome.

jokes.text: If you're starting another story, at least let me finish mine.
jokes.setup: Joey interrupting Rachel's story with his own news
jokes.punchline: Rachel's polite but frustrated attempt to maintain story ownership
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Rachel's assertiveness but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So anyway, I was at the coffee shop and—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh! That reminds me, I got the callback!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That's great, Joey, but I was just—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: And the casting director said I was perfect for the part!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey. Joey, I'm really happy for you, but can I just finish my story?

jokes.text: It's the same story. It's really long.
jokes.setup: Seeming conflict over interrupting stories
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're actually the same story, just extended
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that recontextualizes the previous exchange efficiently

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, you're interrupting my story about the time I ran into my ex at the coffee shop.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, that's MY story! I was there!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You weren't even listening when I started talking about how he was with that blonde girl.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Because I'm the one who saw him first and told YOU about it!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You guys realize you're telling the exact same story, right?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: We are?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, we are.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You've just been arguing about whose story it is for ten minutes.

jokes.text: These really are the 'days of our lives.'
jokes.setup: Joey getting rehired on Days of Our Lives
jokes.punchline: Rachel making the obvious connection to the soap opera title
jokes.explanation: Low-hanging fruit meta reference - obvious and not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're going back on Days of Our Lives?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, that's perfect! Because these really ARE the days of your life!

jokes.text: I got a head rush from standing up too fast.
jokes.setup: Exciting news about jobs and opportunities
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mundane physical complaint as his contribution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deflation of the moment with deadpan mundane observation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I got a job offer today.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm really excited about it. And Joey got an audition for a big commercial.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So, you know, great things are happening for us.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Me? Oh, I've got a really bad back.

jokes.text: How did I do? Was this okay?
jokes.setup: Rachel conducting a job interview
jokes.punchline: Asking the interviewee to evaluate her interviewing performance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Rachel's insecurity in professional situations

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So, do you have any questions for me?

dialogue.speaker: Interviewee
dialogue.line: Actually, yes. How would you rate your interviewing skills?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh! Well, I think I did pretty well. Why?

dialogue.speaker: Interviewee
dialogue.line: Because I'm not sure you asked me a single question about the job.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, didn't I? See, this is exactly why I need feedback!

jokes.text: Hi, yes, I'm sorry. The models are down the hall.
jokes.setup: Tag shows up for assistant interview
jokes.punchline: Rachel assumes he's a model due to his appearance
jokes.explanation: Sets up the visual gag of Tag being unexpectedly attractive for office work

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you're here for the assistant position?

dialogue.speaker: Tag
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's right.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, have you done any... modeling?

jokes.text: Tag Jones. Go on. That's it. That's my whole name.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking for his name
jokes.punchline: Tag's earnest clarification that his unusual name is complete
jokes.explanation: Establishes Tag's earnest, literal character while highlighting his unusual name

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What's your name?

dialogue.speaker: Tag
dialogue.line: Tag.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Tag? Just... Tag?

dialogue.speaker: Tag
dialogue.line: Yeah.

jokes.text: ...two whole summers at TGI Friday's.
jokes.setup: Reading Tag's limited resume
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic emphasis on 'two whole summers' as if it's impressive
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcasm about minimal work experience - functional but not special

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Two whole summers?

jokes.text: Would you stand up?
jokes.setup: Rachel making up HR requirement excuse
jokes.punchline: Obvious pretense to check out Tag's appearance
jokes.explanation: Standard workplace inappropriate behavior comedy - audience sees through the excuse

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I need to see you in my office. It's an HR requirement.

dialogue.speaker: Tag
dialogue.line: Oh, okay.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: We need to do a... full body assessment. For insurance purposes.

jokes.text: No, no, no.
jokes.setup: Tag standing up and presumably looking attractive
jokes.punchline: Rachel's internal struggle with her attraction
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of Rachel fighting her inappropriate attraction

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, he's so hot. No, Rachel, don't go there. He's Tag, your assistant. But look at him. No! Bad Rachel!

jokes.text: We love and respect one another too much.
jokes.setup: Monica saying married people shouldn't have secrets
jokes.punchline: Her sanctimonious tone while immediately contradicting herself
jokes.explanation: Monica's hypocritical high-mindedness before immediately breaking her own rule

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Married people shouldn't have secrets from each other.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's why I haven't told Chandler about the credit card bills.

jokes.text: When I say 'restaurant,' I mean a guy, a hibachi and the trunk of his car.
jokes.setup: Monica describing where they stopped for tacos
jokes.punchline: Clarifying that 'restaurant' is a massive overstatement
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating clarification that paints an increasingly sketchy picture

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We stopped at this taco place on the way back.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, 'place' is a massive overstatement. It was a guy. A guy with a taco cart.

jokes.text: No, he visited a town a little south of throw up.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking if Ross threw up
jokes.punchline: Monica's euphemistic geographic metaphor for defecation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant euphemism with perfect economy - creative way to say something gross

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Did Ross throw up?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, he went to the bathroom.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Let's just say he took a trip to Europe and it wasn't pretty.

jokes.text: Some of the employees decided to rename the park... the 'Crappiest Place on Earth.'
jokes.setup: Ross's bathroom incident at Disneyland
jokes.punchline: Pun on Disney's 'Happiest Place on Earth' slogan
jokes.explanation: Solid pun on famous slogan - predictable but well-executed

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So let me get this straight - you couldn't hold it until you got back to the hotel?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Monica, it was an emergency!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You peed your pants at the Happiest Place on Earth. I guess now it's the Wettest Place on Earth.

jokes.text: That's it? I gave up my Disneyland story for that?
jokes.setup: Trade of embarrassing secrets
jokes.punchline: Chandler realizes he got a bad deal in the secret exchange
jokes.explanation: Good character moment of Chandler's buyer's remorse over secret trading

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You told everyone about the time I cried at that insurance commercial?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, and I only told you about the time I forgot my lines during that audition.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's it? That's your big secret? Joey, everyone forgets their lines. I'm the guy who sobbed uncontrollably at a Geico ad!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I got completely ripped off in this deal.

jokes.text: That's right. You lose, sucker!
jokes.setup: Monica winning the secret exchange
jokes.punchline: Her competitive gloating over trivial victory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica competitiveness over something completely meaningless

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I won! I actually won! Do you know what this means? I'm officially the best gift-giver in this group. The best. That's me now. Monica Geller, champion of Secret Santa.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And don't even try to tell me it was luck. This was skill. This was strategy. I studied each of you for months preparing for this moment.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, remember when you said you liked that pen? I remembered. I REMEMBERED, people!

jokes.text: Please still marry me.
jokes.setup: Monica gloating about winning
jokes.punchline: Immediate vulnerability and need for reassurance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica - competitive then immediately insecure about consequences

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I won! I finally won! I'm the best!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait... you think I'm the best, right? You all think I'm the best?

jokes.text: He's so pretty I want to cry.
jokes.setup: Rachel describing why she wants to hire Tag
jokes.punchline: Her extremely unprofessional, emotional reaction to his appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures shallow attraction with emotional hyperbole

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: He's very... he's very smart. And he's really good with computers. Plus, he's... he's...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: He's really, really, really ridiculously good-looking.

jokes.text: Look how pretty.
jokes.setup: Phoebe giving professional advice about hiring
jokes.punchline: Rachel's simple, childlike counterargument based purely on looks
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - reducing all professional considerations to pure aesthetics

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know, when you're hiring someone, you really need to look at their qualifications, their experience, their references...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: But Phoebe, what if they're really, really hot?

jokes.text: Dumb, old, perfect-for-the-job Hilda.
jokes.setup: Rachel agreeing to hire the qualified candidate
jokes.punchline: Her resentful description revealing her true feelings
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Rachel's petty resentment of the qualified choice

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yes, she's very qualified. She went to Yale, she speaks five languages, and she's only asking for half the salary I was paying the last girl.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: But apparently she has a really annoying laugh and sleeps with her shoes on.

jokes.text: Don't show this to Monica.
jokes.setup: Phoebe looking at Tag's photo
jokes.punchline: Her immediate attraction and need to hide it from Monica
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction to attractive person - sets up later complications

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, who is that?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's Tag, my new assistant at work.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: He's... he's very... I mean, he seems nice. For someone who's probably really dumb.

jokes.text: Funny, these halls look smaller than they used to. It's a different building.
jokes.setup: Joey making nostalgic observation
jokes.punchline: Terry's deadpan correction that it's not the same place
jokes.explanation: Good Joey stupidity moment with efficient straight-man response

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Remember when we used to come here all the time? This place brings back so many memories.

dialogue.speaker: Terry
dialogue.line: Yeah, except this is a completely different restaurant that opened three months ago.

jokes.text: Who looks more like me than me, right?
jokes.setup: Joey explaining why he should get the twin brother role
jokes.punchline: His circular logic about self-resemblance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - technically correct but missing the point of acting

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm perfect for the twin brother role because I look exactly like myself.

jokes.text: This is one star whose hoop... This is the star the hoop... This hoop... I was Dr. Drake Ramoray!
jokes.setup: Joey trying to deliver an indignant exit speech
jokes.punchline: His complete failure to construct a coherent metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent physical/verbal comedy of Joey's brain completely failing under pressure

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm leaving! And I'm taking my talent with me!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You guys are like... you're like a... like a boat.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No wait, you're like a bad... a bad sandwich that I ate.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: And I'm the... I'm the guy who's not hungry anymore!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So... goodbye!

jokes.text: So I guess I shouldn't put 'good at noticing stuff' on my résumé.
jokes.setup: Tag failing to notice obvious plant in office
jokes.punchline: His understated acknowledgment of his observational failure
jokes.explanation: Nice self-deprecating humor that establishes Tag as likeable despite his limitations

dialogue.speaker: Tag
dialogue.line: I didn't notice that plant was there.

jokes.text: Really? No, handshake.
jokes.setup: Tag going in for a hug after getting hired
jokes.punchline: Rachel awkwardly correcting the inappropriate celebration
jokes.explanation: Standard workplace boundary comedy - functional but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Tag
dialogue.line: Thank you so much for hiring me!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Tag, no, no, no. We don't hug. We're not friends. You work for me now.

jokes.text: Always nicer to hear than, 'Oh, crap, you again.'
jokes.setup: Monica saying it's nice he's home
jokes.punchline: Ross's matter-of-fact comparison to a worse alternative
jokes.explanation: Mild self-deprecating humor about low expectations for his presence

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's nice to have you home.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, I could be in a ditch somewhere.

jokes.text: Did you not understand the story?
jokes.setup: Monica making tacos after the bathroom disaster story
jokes.punchline: Ross's incredulous reaction to her food choice
jokes.explanation: Good callback comedy - Ross can't believe she's serving the problem food

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm making tacos!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Tacos? After that story?

jokes.text: They're just... They're ground beef smileys.
jokes.setup: Ross calling out that they're tacos
jokes.punchline: Monica's desperate attempt to rebrand them as something innocent
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy of Monica trying to save face with ridiculous euphemism

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Those are tacos!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, they're not! They're... they're taco-adjacent.

jokes.text: Excuse me, Mr. Mexico.
jokes.setup: Ross identifying tacos correctly
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic dismissal of his taco expertise
jokes.explanation: Monica's defensive sarcasm when caught - character consistent but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh wow, Ross knows what a taco is. What's next, you're going to tell us you can identify a pizza?

jokes.text: It's not like I said, 'What would make this ride more fun?'
jokes.setup: Ross defending his bathroom incident
jokes.punchline: Imagining he deliberately chose to have an accident for entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the embarrassing story with absurd hypothetical

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I didn't deliberately have an accident! Why would I choose to do that? 'Oh, I know what'll be fun today, let me soil myself for entertainment!'

jokes.text: I should be laying down papers for you.
jokes.setup: Chandler apologizing for laughing at Ross's story
jokes.punchline: Suggesting he should treat Ross like a house-training puppy
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler - taking the embarrassing story to its logical insulting conclusion

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, I'm sorry. It's just... you know what? Maybe I should treat you like a house-training puppy. Every time you say something funny, I'll give you a treat.

jokes.text: Now, you're thinking 'He's not the type to go to bars and make out with girls.' And you're right. He's not the type... to go to bars and make out with girls.
jokes.setup: Setting up expectation about Chandler's type
jokes.punchline: The pause reveals he's talking about making out with guys instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect timing and structure

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So what's your type?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, you know, I like a woman who's independent, intelligent, has a good sense of humor...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: ...guys.

jokes.text: In my defense, it was dark and he was a very pretty guy.
jokes.setup: Chandler admitting to kissing a guy
jokes.punchline: His practical justification focusing on attractiveness and lighting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler defense - logical, specific, and completely missing the point

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Look, it was dark, he was attractive, and I was going through a phase!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A phase? Chandler, you're in your thirties.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A long phase!

jokes.text: So hard we had to throw out your underwear again?
jokes.setup: Monica saying she laughed so hard
jokes.punchline: Ross connecting it back to his bathroom incident
jokes.explanation: Good callback that brings the embarrassment full circle

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I laughed so hard!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, that's nothing compared to the time I got stuck in the bathroom at Central Perk.

jokes.text: Whatever, dude. You kissed a guy.
jokes.setup: Complex explanations and justifications
jokes.punchline: Joey's simple, dismissive summary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - cutting through complexity with blunt simplicity

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't understand a word you just said, but I'm gonna go with it.

jokes.text: No, because goats can't talk.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if favorite animals say much about you
jokes.punchline: Joey's literal interpretation about animal communication
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback showing Joey's consistent literal thinking - perfect character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Do you think your favorite animal says something about you?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, like, my favorite animal is the penguin. And I like to think that says I'm cool and I'm a good swimmer.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Hmm, interesting.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Although penguins can't really talk, so I guess it doesn't say much.

jokes.text: Canceled! Like they're taking it off the air? We're not even shooting them anymore?
jokes.setup: Joey getting news about his show being canceled
jokes.punchline: His confused questions showing he doesn't understand what 'canceled' means
jokes.explanation: Good Joey stupidity - genuinely confused by basic industry terminology

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, what do you mean the show's canceled? Like, they're just not gonna air it?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But I already learned all my lines!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Can't they just, like, un-cancel it?

jokes.text: Ross came in fourth and cried!
jokes.setup: Chandler winning Vanilla Ice look-alike contest
jokes.punchline: Ross not only losing but placing poorly and having emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and character insight - Ross's competitive nature and emotional fragility

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I won the Vanilla Ice look-alike contest!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? How did you... I didn't even place!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You didn't place? Ross, there were only five people in the contest.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know! And I still came in last! I looked more like Vanilla Ice than anyone here!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ross, you came in sixth.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: *voice breaking* There were only five people...

jokes.text: I welled up.
jokes.setup: Being accused of crying over contest loss
jokes.punchline: Ross's defensive euphemism for crying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross - trying to maintain dignity with semantic distinctions

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I was not crying. I was just... leaking.

jokes.text: ...so she ate the macaroni off a jewelry box she'd made.
jokes.setup: Monica being sent to bed without dinner
jokes.punchline: Her resourceful but pathetic solution
jokes.explanation: Excellent character revelation - Monica's food obsession and childhood resourcefulness

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm going to bed without dinner.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait... I can just go to the kitchen and make myself something to eat.

jokes.text: It's hard for some people!
jokes.setup: Being teased about not telling time until 13
jokes.punchline: Her defensive rationalization of an embarrassing limitation
jokes.explanation: Good character defense mechanism - Monica making her limitation universal

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I was a late bloomer, okay? Some people don't learn to tell time until they're older.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, you were thirteen.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, analog clocks are confusing! There's two hands and they move at different speeds. It's not intuitive!

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I couldn't think of any more for Ross.
jokes.setup: Revealing Chandler wore her underwear
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical explanation for switching targets
jokes.explanation: Good Monica organization - even her revenge is systematic and practical

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, you wore my underwear?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It was an emergency!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What kind of emergency requires my underwear?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I ran out of clean ones!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So you just thought, 'I'll borrow Monica's'? You could've asked!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Would you have said yes?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No! Which is exactly why you should've asked. Then I would've known to hide them.

jokes.text: Whatever, dude. You kissed a guy.
jokes.setup: Complex embarrassing story about Ross
jokes.punchline: Joey's repeated dismissive summary
jokes.explanation: Effective running gag - Joey's consistent reductive perspective

jokes.text: You? An actor? That's madness!
jokes.setup: Joey complaining about having to audition
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sarcastic shock at the concept of an actor auditioning
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic takedown of Joey's entitlement with efficient delivery

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I have to go on another audition today. I hate auditioning.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What? An actor has to audition? That's insane!

jokes.text: Really?
jokes.setup: Tag claiming he was 'just practicing' answering the phone
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dubious reaction to his obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Basic skeptical reaction - functional but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, sure you were.

jokes.text: So you hired yourself a little treat, did you?
jokes.setup: Phoebe seeing Tag
jokes.punchline: Her knowing assessment of Rachel's hiring motivation
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe directness - cutting through pretense with blunt observation

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, you hired him because he's hot!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, I didn't!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, you did. It's okay, I do it all the time. I once hired a guy just because he had a tattoo of a scorpion on his neck.

jokes.text: Hey, cute assistant. What's his story? Is he... Gay? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Joanna asking about Tag
jokes.punchline: Rachel immediately lying about his sexuality
jokes.explanation: Standard protective lying - sets up later complications but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Joanna
dialogue.line: So what's the deal with Tag? Is he gay?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, yes. Completely gay. Super gay. The gayest guy I know.

jokes.text: Surprised your big head could fit through our small halls.
jokes.setup: Joey returning after his outburst
jokes.punchline: Terry's pointed reference to Joey's ego and earlier confusion about building size
jokes.explanation: Good callback with literal/metaphorical wordplay about head size

dialogue.speaker: Terry
dialogue.line: So, you're back. Ready to apologize for calling me a liar about the building being twelve stories?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Terry, come on, I didn't mean—

dialogue.speaker: Terry
dialogue.line: No, no, it's fine. I'm used to it. Everyone knows buildings can't be twelve stories tall. Joey Tribbiani said so.

jokes.text: Time to kiss a guy?
jokes.setup: Monica asking what time it is
jokes.punchline: Chandler deflecting with reference to his embarrassing secret
jokes.explanation: Good callback deflection - avoiding the clock reading embarrassment with different embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What time is it?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's, uh... it's time to stop asking me questions about my personal life.

jokes.text: What are you laughing at, Pampers?
jokes.setup: Monica laughing at his deflection
jokes.punchline: Chandler's retort referencing her childhood bedwetting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of embarrassing nickname warfare

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hey, at least I didn't wet the bed until I was a teenager.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I was seven!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And it was the most magical night of my life.

jokes.text: Then I'd steer clear of Phoebe.
jokes.setup: Ross wanting to avoid people who know the Space Mountain story
jokes.punchline: Monica revealing Phoebe also knows
jokes.explanation: Standard callback showing the story's spread - functional but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, and Phoebe knows too.

jokes.text: Yeah, she's gay.
jokes.setup: Tag wanting to ask out Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Rachel using the same lie she told about him
jokes.explanation: Excellent symmetrical callback - Rachel trapped by her own lie and using it defensively again

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: He's not available. He's dating someone.

dialogue.speaker: Tag
dialogue.line: What? Who?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Me.

tmdb_overview: While hiring a new assistant, Rachel is torn between hiring a much more qualified woman, or a handsome but inexperienced young man. Monica, Chandler and Ross engage in a war of secret sharing after Monica and Chandler vow to be more honest. Meanwhile, Joey has problems when his TV show is cancelled after just one airing.
insight: Rachel's career arc delivers 58 jokes in 22 minutes, but lacks the punchy peaks of stronger season entries.
